intent.\n- The bytecode is a long hex string, which is standard for compiled contracts, not obfuscated or malicious.\n- The code does not contain any network calls, file operations, or data leaks.\n- All reports agree that the code appears benign and standard.\n\n---\n\n### Issue Presence and Logic\n\n**Issue detection:**  \n- The code does **not** process untrusted input, nor does it contain any suspicious logic or behavior.  \n- The functions are standard for deploying or connecting to a known contract interface.  \n- No anomalies, obfuscation, or malicious patterns are present.\n\n**Conclusion:**  \n- No issues are present in the code itself.\n\n---\n\n### Evaluation of the Scores\n\n**Malware Score:**  \n- All reports correctly assign a malware score of 0, as straightforward, autogenerated nature and lack of obfuscation.\n\n**Security Risk Score:**  \n- All reports assign very low scores (0 or 0.1).  \n- The code does not handle external data, secrets, or the analysis.**\n\n---\n\n### Justification for any higher scores:\n\n- No evidence of malware or obfuscation justifies increasing the malware or obfuscated scores.\n- The minimal security risk score
